scenario_title: delete_rr of an absent RR after a passing prerequisite

group_1.behavior: Treats the delete_rr for an absent RR as a benign no-op once prerequisites have passed, and completes the UPDATE successfully.
group_1.post_state: host2.campus.edu. remains absent; host1.campus.edu. A remains present.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE or aborts it when asked to delete a specific RR that is not present, despite the prerequisite succeeding.
group_2.post_state: host2.campus.edu. remains absent; host1.campus.edu. A remains present.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4 describes delete_rr syntax and semantics, but does not clearly specify whether deleting a nonexistent RR after all prerequisites succeed must be treated as a harmless no-op or as an error that aborts the UPDATE. Section 7.2 discusses prerequisite failure handling, but leaves this update-side absence case underspecified, so implementations may diverge on both the reply code and whether the transaction is considered successful.
expected_divergence.test_rationale: The zone contains one existing helper RRset so the prerequisite can succeed, while the UPDATE targets a different name whose specific A RR is absent. This isolates the underspecified behavior of delete_rr on a missing RR from prerequisite handling. The follow-up queries make it possible to distinguish a server that silently accepts the UPDATE from one that rejects or aborts it.
